tools.description: Explicitly attach to a running browser via CDP endpoint. Requires an explicit target (--cdp, --endpoint, or --port) and never falls back to launch. Alias for bc_browser_attach.

endpoint.description: CDP endpoint URL alias (same as cdp).

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

targetType.description: Target type hint: chrome, chromium, msedge, electron, or unknown.

tools.name: attach

tools.description: Explicitly attach to a running browser via CDP endpoint. Requires an explicit target (--cdp, --endpoint, or --port) and never falls back to launch.

endpoint.description: CDP endpoint URL alias (same as cdp).

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

targetType.description: Target type hint: chrome, chromium, msedge, electron, or unknown.

tools.name: bc_browser_attach

tools.description: Click an element on the page. Target can be a ref (@e3), CSS selector, or semantic text match. Prefer refs from a snapshot for stability.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

target.description: Element to click: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

tools.name: bc_browser_click

tools.description: Close the browser automation lifecycle. Managed Chrome is terminated; attached Chrome is detached without killing the user's browser.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_close

tools.description: Detach from the current browser connection without closing attached browsers. For managed browsers, this is a no-op (use close instead). Returns structured detach result.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_detach

tools.description: List recent downloads for the current session. Returns structured download information including filename, path, size, and status.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_downloads_list

tools.description: Drop files or data onto a page element. Supports file drop (local files) and data drop (MIME/value pairs like text/plain). Target can be a ref (@e3), CSS selector, or semantic text match.

data.description: MIME/value pairs for clipboard-like data drop (e.g., text/plain=hello).

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

target.description: Element to drop onto: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

tools.name: bc_browser_drop

tools.description: Fill a form field with text. Target can be a ref (@e3), CSS selector, or semantic text match. Use commit:true to press Tab after filling.

commit.description: Press Tab after filling to move to the next field.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

target.description: Element to fill: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

tools.name: bc_browser_fill

tools.description: Generate stable locator candidates for a target element. Returns ordered candidates by confidence (role/name, label, placeholder, text, testid, css, xpath).

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

target.description: Element ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match to generate locators for.

tools.name: bc_browser_generate_locator

tools.description: Highlight a target element visually on the page. Injects a temporary overlay that auto-removes after 5 seconds unless persist is true.

hide.description: Whether to hide the highlight (if true, removes all highlights).

persist.description: Whether to persist the highlight (default: false).

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

target.description: Element to highlight: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

tools.name: bc_browser_highlight

tools.description: Hover over an element. Target can be a ref (@e3), CSS selector, or semantic text match.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

target.description: Element to hover: ref (@e3), CSS selector, or text match.

tools.name: bc_browser_hover

tools.description: List attachable browsers on the local system. Probes CDP endpoints and returns browser metadata including channel, endpoint, and attachment status.

all.description: List all discovered browsers including those not currently attached.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_list

tools.description: Open a URL in the browser. If no browser is connected, attempts to attach to a running browser or launch a managed automation profile.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_open

tools.description: Press a keyboard key (e.g., 'Enter', 'Tab', 'ArrowDown', 'Escape').

key.description: Key to press: Enter, Tab, ArrowDown, ArrowUp, ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, Escape, Backspace, etc.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_press

tools.description: List available browser providers and the active provider.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_provider_list

tools.description: Set the active browser provider.

name.description: Provider name to activate (local, custom, browserless).

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_provider_use

tools.description: Start a browser screencast recording. Opt-in recording with action timeline support.

annotationPosition.description: Position for action labels: top-left, top, top-right, bottom-left, bottom, bottom-right.

path.description: Optional output file path for the screencast.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

showActions.description: Display visible action labels during recording.

tools.name: bc_browser_screencast_start

tools.description: Get the current screencast recording status.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_screencast_status

tools.description: Stop the current screencast recording and save the action timeline receipt.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_screencast_stop

tools.description: Take a screenshot of the page or a specific element.

annotate.description: Annotate screenshot with ref labels and boxes for interactive elements.

fullPage.description: Capture the full page instead of just the viewport.

outputPath.description: Optional custom screenshot file path. If omitted, Browser Control saves under the active session runtime screenshots directory.

refs.description: Comma-separated list of specific refs to annotate (if annotate is true). If omitted, annotates all interactive elements.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

target.description: Element ref or selector to screenshot. If omitted, screenshots the viewport.

tools.name: bc_browser_screenshot

tools.description: Scroll the page in a direction.

direction.description: Direction to scroll: up, down, left, right.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_scroll

tools.description: Take an accessibility snapshot of the current page. Returns a structured representation with stable element refs (e.g., @e1, @e2) for semantic interaction. Use this before clicking or filling elements.

boxes.description: Include element bounds with viewport metadata in the snapshot.

rootSelector.description: Optional CSS selector to scope the snapshot to a subtree.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_snapshot

tools.description: Close the current browser tab. Keeps the browser session and automation lifecycle alive.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_tab_close

tools.description: List all browser tabs with their IDs, URLs, and titles.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_tab_list

tools.description: Switch to a browser tab by index.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_tab_switch

delayMs.description: Delay between keystrokes in milliseconds. Default: 0.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.

tools.name: bc_browser_type

tools.description: Retrieve a debug bundle by ID. Debug bundles contain structured evidence about failed actions including browser state, terminal output, console logs, network events, and recovery guidance.

bundleId.description: The debug bundle ID to retrieve.

tools.name: bc_debug_failure_bundle

tools.description: Get captured browser console entries for a session. Returns log, warn, error, info, and debug entries with timestamps and source locations.

sessionId.description: Session ID to get console entries for. Default: 'default'.

tools.name: bc_debug_get_console

tools.description: Get captured browser network entries for a session. Returns request failures and HTTP errors with URL, method, status, and duration.

sessionId.description: Session ID to get network entries for. Default: 'default'.

tools.name: bc_debug_get_network

tools.description: Run health checks on the Browser Control runtime: CDP connectivity, memory store, proxy pool, CAPTCHA config, OpenRouter config, disk space, browser state, data directories, config validity, daemon broker, and system memory. Returns an overall health status and per-check results.

tools.description: Type text into the currently focused element (e.g., after clicking a textbox). Alias for bc_browser_type.

delayMs.description: Delay between keystrokes in milliseconds. Default: 0.

sessionId.description: Browser Control session ID. If omitted, uses the active session.
